metadata.summary: Implement async polling, error handling, and agentic workflow patterns for AI video generation APIs (Runway, Luma, Replicate) where all providers are async with 60-300s latency.

content.title: AI Video Generation Async API Patterns
content.summary: All AI video generation providers are async with 60-300s generation latency. Agents must never block synchronously — use polling with exponential backoff, timeout guards, and immediate video download after completion since provider URLs expire in 24-72h.

why.p: Video generation APIs (Runway, Luma, Replicate) return a job ID on submission and require polling a separate status endpoint. Generation takes 60-300 seconds depending on provider, duration, and load. Blocking synchronously freezes the agent thread. URL expiry (24-72h) means the agent must download the video file immediately after completion — storing only the URL leads to broken links in production. Silent FAILED states (status: FAILED with empty failure message) require defensive logging of every raw response.

list.item: Integrating any video generation provider into an agent or service pipeline.
Building a polling loop for Runway Gen-3, Luma Dream Machine, or Replicate video models.
Implementing parallel multi-clip generation with ffmpeg assembly.
Setting up agentic workflow: provider selection, job submission, poll, download, downstream handoff.
Real-time generation needed (under 30s turnaround) — no current provider supports this; re-evaluate use case.
Synchronous architecture where the caller cannot tolerate async callbacks or polling — restructure to async first.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: A subagent receives a script or text prompt, selects the appropriate provider based on task requirements (lip-sync: Runway; long-form: Sora; budget: Luma), submits the generation job, polls for completion with exponential backoff, downloads the video file, and passes it to a downstream agent for assembly or publishing. For multi-clip projects, the agent generates clips in parallel and assembles with ffmpeg. Human review is required before publishing any generated video.

rule.statement: Video generation agents MUST poll with a sleep interval (minimum 5s, recommended 10s) and a hard timeout (recommended 10 minutes / 600s). Never block synchronously waiting for a video generation job.
rule.rationale: All providers are async with 60-300s latency. Blocking synchronously freezes agent threads and wastes compute. A hard timeout prevents infinite hangs when providers silently fail.

rule.statement: After receiving a SUCCEEDED status, download the video file immediately to local or persistent storage. Do NOT store provider-issued URLs as the final artifact — Runway and Luma URLs expire in 24-72h.
rule.rationale: Provider-issued video URLs expire, typically within 24-72h. Storing only the URL leads to broken references in downstream pipelines and content delivery.

rule.statement: Always log the full raw API response on every poll cycle, including FAILED states. Generation failures can be silent (status: FAILED with empty failure_message field).
rule.rationale: Silent failures with empty error messages are common across providers. Without the full raw response logged, debugging production failures becomes impossible.

rule.statement: Implement per-provider rate limiting when generating multiple clips in parallel. Use provider-specific concurrency limits and add per-provider semaphores or queue guards.
rule.rationale: Parallel generation of many clips may hit provider rate limits (429 errors). Without per-provider rate limiting, all parallel jobs fail simultaneously and retry storms amplify the problem.

section.name: runway-polling-example
section.p: Runway Gen-3 API — async job submission with polling. The submission endpoint and status polling endpoint are separate.

example.title: Runway Gen-3 async polling with timeout
example.note: Runway returns task ID on submission; status polling is a separate GET endpoint. Loop limit of 60 x 10s = 10 minutes hard cap.

example.title: Blocking synchronous call — do not do this
example.code: # DO NOT: blocking synchronous call that waits indefinitely import runwayml client = runwayml.RunwayML() task = client.image_to_video.create(model="gen3a_turbo", prompt_text=prompt) # waiting without timeout — agent thread hangs for 60-300s while task.status not in ("SUCCEEDED", "FAILED"): task = client.tasks.retrieve(task.id)
example.note: No timeout guard; if provider hangs, agent thread freezes indefinitely.

section.name: sdk-clients
section.p: CLI tools and SDK clients for video generation:

list.item: runwayml Python SDK: Runway Gen-3 API. Install: pip install runwayml. Docs: runwayml.com/api.
lumaai Python SDK: Luma Dream Machine API. Install: pip install lumaai. Docs: lumalabs.ai/api.
replicate Python SDK: Open-source video models (CogVideoX, AnimateDiff). Install: pip install replicate. Docs: replicate.com.
ffmpeg: Video assembly, format conversion, concatenation. Install: system package. Docs: ffmpeg.org.
moviepy: Python video editing (trim, overlay, concat). Install: pip install moviepy. Docs: zulko.github.io/moviepy.
httpx: Async HTTP for provider polling loops. Install: pip install httpx. Docs: python-httpx.org.
Runway ML (SaaS): Yes — Public REST API; Gen-3 Alpha; lip-sync; video-to-video.
Luma AI (SaaS): Yes — Public API; Dream Machine; image animation; video extension.
Replicate (SaaS): Yes — API for 50+ open video models; pay per generation.
Kling (SaaS): Yes — Credits-based API; 10s at 1080p; competitive quality.
Stability AI Stable Video (SaaS): Yes — Image-to-video; lower cost than Runway.
OpenAI Sora (SaaS): Partial — API limited to ChatGPT Pro subscribers as of 2026.
Google Veo 3 (SaaS): Partial — Via Vertex AI; enterprise access required for 4K.

section.name: multi-clip-parallel-assembly
section.p: For multi-clip projects, generate clips in parallel and assemble with ffmpeg concat.

example.title: Parallel multi-clip generation and ffmpeg concat
example.note: Generate at provider-native duration (5-10s); assemble longer videos with ffmpeg concat. Cache video files by prompt hash — regeneration costs the same.

section.name: gotchas
section.p: AI-agent gotchas for video generation APIs:

list.item: All providers are async with 60-300s latency; agent must never block synchronously — use polling with timeout.
Runway returns a task ID on submission; status polling endpoint is separate from submission endpoint.
Generation failures can be silent (status: FAILED with empty failure message) — always log raw response.
Parallel generation of many clips may hit provider rate limits; implement per-provider rate limiting.
Video URLs from Runway/Luma expire (typically 24-72h) — download immediately after completion, do not store URLs.
Human review checkpoint before any public video publication — AI video can contain unexpected content.

section.name: best-practices
section.p: Best practices for video generation API integration:

list.item: Always specify "no text, no logos" explicitly in prompts to avoid content policy triggers and visual noise.
Seed with a reference image (image-to-video) for character consistency — text-only produces higher variance.
Generate at provider-native duration (5-10s); assemble longer videos with ffmpeg concat.
Cache video files by prompt hash — regeneration costs the same; avoid unnecessary regeneration.
Monitor per-generation cost: add a cost estimate log before every job submission.
Never publish AI-generated video without a review step for accuracy, brand fit, and policy compliance.

checklist.check: Implement async polling with minimum 5-10s interval and 10-minute hard timeout.
Download video immediately after SUCCEEDED status — do not rely on provider URLs long-term.
Log full raw API response including FAILED states with empty error messages.
Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for transient errors.
Add per-provider rate limiting for parallel generation workflows.
Handle 429 rate limiting, content moderation rejections, and silent FAILED states.
Add idempotency keys to prevent duplicate submissions on retry.
Implement human review checkpoint before any public video publication.
